---
title: "Troubleshooting"
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

## Troubleshooting

## App Shows a Database Error

Initialize the MS database:

```bash
cd /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

If data already exists and you only want schema/seed checks:

```bash
sudo -u shiny MS_RESET_DB=0 Rscript setup_database.R
```

Check ownership:

```bash
ls -l /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db
```

## `/ms-app/` Returns 404

Check that Shiny serves the app:

```bash
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3838/ms-app/
```

Check Apache proxy paths in `scripts/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf`:

```apache
ProxyPass /ms-app/ http://127.0.0.1:3838/ms-app/
ProxyPassReverse /ms-app/ http://127.0.0.1:3838/ms-app/
```

Restart:

```bash
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

## LDAP Prompt Works but App Does Not Log In

This is the same failure mode documented for BCFNGS: Apache LDAP works, but
the Shiny R worker is still running with local-mode environment settings.
Do not rely only on `systemctl show shiny-server --property=Environment`;
the working BCFNGS setup uses the app-local `.Renviron`.

Confirm the app-local runtime mode:

```bash
sudo cat /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
```

Required:

```ini
AUTH_MODE=ldap
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
```

If needed, reset it exactly:

```bash
sudo tee /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron >/dev/null <<'EOF'
AUTH_MODE=ldap
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
LDAP_DEBUG=1
EOF
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo chmod 640 /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

Verify that the deployed code is current:

```bash
sudo grep -n "client_url_search\\|auth-status-box\\|is_ldap_mode" /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/app.R
```

Expected: all three terms should be found. If not, redeploy the latest `ms-app/`.

Then inspect the latest Shiny log:

```bash
LATEST=$(sudo ls -1t /var/log/shiny-server/ms-app-shiny-*.log | head -n1)
sudo tail -n 120 "$LATEST"
```

Check the redirect:

```bash
curl -Ik -u USER https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/ms-app/
```

Expected: `Location: https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/ms-app/?auth_user=USER`.

## Upload Pool Missing

Configured root:

```text
/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs-ms-projects
```

If this path does not exist, the app stores uploads in `uploads_pending_pool/` under the app directory and logs/sends a facility warning. This is intentional; project submission should not stop because the pool folder is not mounted yet.

Check fallback files:

```bash
sudo find /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/uploads_pending_pool -maxdepth 2 -type f
```

## Logs

```bash
sudo tail -n 120 /var/log/shiny-server/ms-app-shiny-*.log
sudo tail -n 120 /var/log/apache2/ms-app-ssl-access.log
sudo tail -n 120 /var/log/apache2/ms-app-ssl-error.log
```

## Local Maintenance Mode

```bash
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=local/' /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

Return to LDAP:

```bash
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=ldap/' /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```
